His bid for the [Triple Crown](/wiki/British_Fillies_Triple_Crown "British Fillies Triple Crown") failed narrowly when he finished runner-up in the [St Leger](/wiki/St_Leger_Stakes "St Leger Stakes").

**Camelot** (foaled 15 March 2009) is a British-bred, Irish-trained [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") racehorse. He was one of the leading European two-year-olds of 2011 and won the [Racing Post Trophy](/wiki/Racing_Post_Trophy "Racing Post Trophy") at [Doncaster](/wiki/Doncaster_Racecourse "Doncaster Racecourse"). On his three-year-old debut in 2012, Camelot won the 2000 Guineas at [Newmarket](/wiki/Newmarket_Racecourse "Newmarket Racecourse") and followed up by winning the Derby at [Epsom](/wiki/Epsom_Downs_Racecourse "Epsom Downs Racecourse") and the Irish Derby at the [Curragh](/wiki/Curragh_Racecourse "Curragh Racecourse"). His bid for the [Triple Crown](/wiki/British_Fillies_Triple_Crown "British Fillies Triple Crown") failed narrowly when he finished runner-up in the [St Leger](/wiki/St_Leger_Stakes "St Leger Stakes").

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Camelot is a dark-coated bay horse with an [interrupted white stripe](/wiki/Horse_markings "Horse markings"), bred by [Sheikh Abdulla Bin Isa Al-Khalifa](/wiki/Abdullah_Bin_Hamad_Bin_Isa_Al-Khalifa "Abdullah Bin Hamad Bin Isa Al-Khalifa"). The colt was reared at the Highclere Stud near [Newbury](/wiki/Newbury,_Berkshire "Newbury, Berkshire") in Berkshire, where his "paddock playmate" was a foal who went on to be named Bonfire.[[1]](#cite_note-guardian1-1) In October 2010, Camelot was sent as a yearling to the Tattersalls sales at [Newmarket](/wiki/Newmarket,_Suffolk "Newmarket, Suffolk"), where he was bought for 525,000 [guineas](/wiki/Guinea_(coin) "Guinea (coin)") by the bloodstock agent Dermot "Demi" O'Byrne[[2]](#cite_note-2) on behalf of the [Coolmore](/wiki/Coolmore_Stud "Coolmore Stud") organisation. Like most Coolmore horses, he was sent into training with [Aidan O'Brien](/wiki/Aidan_O%27Brien "Aidan O'Brien") at [Ballydoyle](/wiki/Ballydoyle "Ballydoyle"). For racing purposes, Camelot is registered as being owned by Derrick Smith, [Mrs John Magnier](/wiki/John_Magnier "John Magnier") and [Michael Tabor](/wiki/Michael_Tabor "Michael Tabor").

Camelot is one of many top-class horses sired by [Montjeu](/wiki/Montjeu "Montjeu"). Others include the [Derby](/wiki/Epsom_Derby "Epsom Derby") winners [Motivator](/wiki/Motivator_(horse) "Motivator (horse)"), [Authorized](/wiki/Authorized_(horse) "Authorized (horse)") and [Pour Moi](/wiki/Pour_Moi_(horse) "Pour Moi (horse)"), the [St Leger](/wiki/St._Leger_Stakes "St. Leger Stakes") winners [Scorpion](/wiki/Scorpion_(horse) "Scorpion (horse)") and [Masked Marvel](/wiki/Masked_Marvel_(horse) "Masked Marvel (horse)"), and the [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") winner [Hurricane Run](/wiki/Hurricane_Run "Hurricane Run").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Camelot's dam, the [Kentucky](/wiki/Kentucky "Kentucky")-bred Tarfah, was a good racemare who won the [Group Three](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [Dahlia Stakes](/wiki/Dahlia_Stakes "Dahlia Stakes") in 2004.

Aidan O'Brien, commenting on the horse's earliest appearance in the exercise said, "With him it's natural, it's the way he is. His movement is perfection"[[4]](#cite_note-telegraph2000-4)

## Racing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Racing career")]

### 2011: two-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2011: two-year-old season")]

Camelot made his first appearance in a maiden race at [Leopardstown](/wiki/Leopardstown_Racecourse "Leopardstown Racecourse") on 14 July 2011. Starting at odds of [1/3](/wiki/Fractional_odds "Fractional odds"), he was restrained in the early stages by Joseph O'Brien (his trainer's son) before taking the lead in the final [furlong](/wiki/Furlong "Furlong") and winning "easily" by two [lengths](/wiki/Horse_length "Horse length") from All Approved.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Despite the modest nature of the race,[[6]](#cite_note-6) Camelot was immediately made favourite for the following year's Epsom Derby. He was then moved directly into Group One class as he made his next appearance in the [Racing Post Trophy](/wiki/Racing_Post_Trophy "Racing Post Trophy") at [Doncaster](/wiki/Doncaster_Racecourse "Doncaster Racecourse"), where he went off the [odds on favourite](/wiki/Gambling "Gambling"). He was again held up by Joseph O'Brien before taking the lead in the closing stages and winning by 2¼ lengths in "very impressive"[[7]](#cite_note-7) style. Following the race, his Derby odds were cut to 3/1.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Aidan O'Brien said that the colt had "looked extraordinary all the way along" and was "unbelievable at home."[[9]](#cite_note-9) He also explained that while most regarded the Derby as the colt's main objective, the 2000 Guineas was also a target.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### 2012: three-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2012: three-year-old season")]

#### Spring

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Spring")]

In early 2012, Camelot maintained his position as favourite for both the 2000 Guineas and the Derby,[[11]](#cite_note-11) despite O'Brien expressing doubts about the colt's participation in the former race.[[12]](#cite_note-12) On his three-year-old debut, Camelot, ridden again by Joseph O'Brien, started the 15/8 favourite the 2000 Guineas at [Newmarket](/wiki/Newmarket_Racecourse "Newmarket Racecourse") on 5 May. After being towards the rear of the field in the early running, he accelerated to take the lead in the closing stages and won by a neck from French Fifteen.[[13]](#cite_note-13) After the race, Aidan O'Brien said, "It's one of those unbelievable days... we always thought he was very special" and indicated that the Derby would probably be Camelot's next race.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Joseph O'Brien, who gave the colt a "cool" and "supremely confident" ride according to *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* said that the race went "pretty much to plan".[[15]](#cite_note-15) Camelot became a shorter-priced favourite for the Derby. O'Brien pointed out that the colt's breeding and temperament made him potentially well-suited for the race but explained that "the thing with Epsom is, you never really know these things until you go there."[[4]](#cite_note-telegraph2000-4)

#### Summer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Summer")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/Father_and_son_walking_the_course.jpg/250px-Father_and_son_walking_the_course.jpg)](/wiki/File:Father_and_son_walking_the_course.jpg)

Aidan (right) and Joseph O'Brien (centre) walk the course before the 2012 Derby with [Seamie Heffernan](/wiki/Seamie_Heffernan "Seamie Heffernan") (left)

On 2 June, Camelot started the 8/13 favourite for the [Epsom Derby](/wiki/2012_Epsom_Derby "2012 Epsom Derby") in front of a crowd of 130,000, which included [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II "Queen Elizabeth II"), who was beginning the celebrations for her [Diamond Jubilee](/wiki/Diamond_Jubilee_of_Queen_Elizabeth_II "Diamond Jubilee of Queen Elizabeth II"). His main rival was expected to be Bonfire, his former paddock companion at Highclere, who had won the [Dante Stakes](/wiki/Dante_Stakes "Dante Stakes"). Other contenders in the field of nine (the smallest since 1907[[16]](#cite_note-16)) included [Main Sequence](/wiki/Main_Sequence_(horse) "Main Sequence (horse)"), the winner of the [Lingfield Derby Trial](/wiki/Lingfield_Derby_Trial "Lingfield Derby Trial"), and Camelot's stable companion Astrology, who had won the [Dee Stakes](/wiki/Dee_Stakes "Dee Stakes") by eleven lengths.[[1]](#cite_note-guardian1-1) Ridden by Joseph O'Brien, Camelot was restrained at the back of the field as Astrology made the running. Camelot moved up to challenge for the lead in the straight and pulled clear to win by five lengths from Main Sequence in second and Astrology in third.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The victory made O'Brien and Joseph, 19, the first father-son/trainer-jockey pairing to win the race.[[18]](#cite_note-18) After the race, Joseph O'Brien explained that the horse had struggled to cope with the descent to the turn into the straight, saying that Camelot "had only run three times before and never on a track like this, so he was looking where to put his feet round Tattenham Corner".[[19]](#cite_note-19) The win gave Aidan O'Brien his fourth [British Classic](/wiki/British_Classic_Races "British Classic Races") win of the season and led to speculation that Camelot could become the first horse since [Nijinsky](/wiki/Nijinsky_II "Nijinsky II") in 1970 to win the Triple Crown.[[20]](#cite_note-20) Since Nijinsky, only two horses have won the 2000 Guineas and the Derby: [Nashwan](/wiki/Nashwan "Nashwan") in 1989 and [Sea the Stars](/wiki/Sea_the_Stars "Sea the Stars") in 2009, neither of whom contested the St Leger. O'Brien said that "every trip he's raced over, he's coped... there are a lot of options and the [St Leger](/wiki/St._Leger_Stakes "St. Leger Stakes") is one."[[21]](#cite_note-21)

On 30 June, Camelot ran in Ireland for only the second time when he contested the Irish Derby at the Curragh. He started the 1/5 favourite against four opponents in a race run on extremely soft ground. Joseph O'Brien settled Camelot in fourth place as Astrology made the running. Astrology weakened half a mile from the finish, and Akeed Mofeed went to the front ahead of Light Heavy. O'Brien moved Camelot up to lead on the outside two furlongs from the finish and held off the late challenge of [Born to Sea](/w/index.php?title=Born_to_Sea&action=edit&redlink=1 "Born to Sea (page does not exist)") to win by two lengths despite hanging to the left in the closing stages.[[22]](#cite_note-22) It was the trainer's seventh consecutive Irish Derby win and tenth in all, and made Camelot the sixteenth horse to complete the Epsom and Irish Derby double.[[23]](#cite_note-23) After the race, Aidan O'Brien said that he had been very worried by the state of the ground before praising the colt's determination and indicating that he would be rested to prepare for an autumn campaign. He described the Triple Crown as "something incredible to dream about".[[24]](#cite_note-24)

#### Autumn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Autumn")]

After the Irish Derby, Camelot was rested until the St Leger at [Doncaster Racecourse](/wiki/Doncaster_Racecourse "Doncaster Racecourse") on 15 September, making him the first horse to attempt to win the Triple Crown since Nijinsky in 1970. He started a 2/5 favourite for the classic, which attracted a crowd of 32,000 to the Yorkshire track. In the race, he was settled towards the rear of the field before moving up on the inside challenge in the straight, where he was briefly unable to obtain a clear run. Camelot made up ground throughout the final quarter-mile but was unable to run down [Encke](/wiki/Encke_(horse) "Encke (horse)"), who had gained a decisive break on the field, and finished second by three-quarters of a length. (In May 2013 Encke tested positive for a banned steroid and there were calls for his disqualification from the 2012 St Leger, which would have made Camelot a Triple Crown winner.) There was some criticism of Joseph O'Brien's riding, but Aidan O'Brien blamed himself for failing to run a pacemaker.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Three weeks after his defeat at Doncaster, Camelot raced against older horses for the first time in the [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") at [Longchamp](/wiki/Longchamp_Racecourse "Longchamp Racecourse"). The colt was set to carry a weight of 123 pounds, too low for Joseph O'Brien, so the ride on Camelot was given to [Frankie Dettori](/wiki/Frankie_Dettori "Frankie Dettori"), a jockey usually associated with Coolmore's rival [Godolphin](/wiki/Godolphin_Racing "Godolphin Racing").[[26]](#cite_note-26) Camelot started second favourite at odds of 3.75/1 but although he made some progress in the straight he never challenged the leaders and finished seventh of the eighteen runners behind [Solemia](/wiki/Solemia "Solemia").[[27]](#cite_note-27) After the race, O'Brien explained that the effects of a long season and the extremely soft ground had contributed to Camelot's run and announced that the colt would stay in training in 2013.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

On 11 October, Camelot became ill with [colic](/wiki/Horse_colic "Horse colic") and was admitted to the Fethard Veterinary Hospital, where he underwent surgery.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In November, Camelot was a clear-cut winner of the vote for the title of [European Champion three-year-old colt](/wiki/Cartier_Champion_Three-year-old_Colt "Cartier Champion Three-year-old Colt") at the [Cartier Racing Awards](/wiki/Cartier_Racing_Awards "Cartier Racing Awards").[[30]](#cite_note-30) In December, he was named Irish Horse of the Year in a poll conducted by [Horse Racing Ireland](/wiki/Horse_Racing_Ireland "Horse Racing Ireland").[[31]](#cite_note-31)

### 2013: four-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2013: four-year-old season")]

Camelot began his third season at the Curragh on 6 May, when he contested the Group 2 [Mooresbridge Stakes](/wiki/Mooresbridge_Stakes "Mooresbridge Stakes") over ten furlongs. He started the 1/3 favourite and won "comfortably" by one and three quarter lengths from his stable companion Triumphant.[[32]](#cite_note-32) On 26 May, Camelot started favourite for the [Tattersalls Gold Cup](/wiki/Tattersalls_Gold_Cup "Tattersalls Gold Cup"), but was beaten by the British five-year-old [Al Kazeem](/wiki/Al_Kazeem "Al Kazeem"). On 20 June, the colt started the 5/2 favourite for the [Prince of Wales's Stakes](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales%27s_Stakes "Prince of Wales's Stakes") at [Royal Ascot](/wiki/Royal_Ascot "Royal Ascot"), but was beaten four lengths, into fourth by Al Kazeem. Camelot remained in training until the autumn but missed intended runs in the [Eclipse Stakes](/wiki/Eclipse_Stakes "Eclipse Stakes"), [Juddmonte International](/wiki/Juddmonte_International "Juddmonte International"), [Irish Champion Stakes](/wiki/Irish_Champion_Stakes "Irish Champion Stakes") and Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe.

### Retirement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Retirement")]

On 14 October 2013, Coolmore announced that Camelot was retiring and would start a breeding career in 2014. O'Brien said that he felt that the horse had never fully recovered after his attack of colic. Paying tribute to the horse O'Brien said "He was the perfect racehorse, a beautiful horse with a terrific action. Speed, class and a super-intelligent horse to go with it – he had it all". In 2018, his stud fee is €30,000.[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)

## Stud career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camelot_(horse)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Stud career")]

Camelot stands as a stallion at [Coolmore Stud](/wiki/Coolmore_Stud "Coolmore Stud"). His fee for the year 2021 stands at €60,000 per service.[[35]](#cite_note-35)
